title: PD 4786
content:
{{Top|leg}}
'''PD 4786''' was the designation of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]] that was deployed over [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] by [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. 

Unlike other viper droids, this had not one, but four [[blaster cannon/Legends|energy cannons]], one at each compass point. It was equipped with [[Holocamera/Legends|Holocameras]] although at nighttime it operated with heat [[sensor/Legends|sensors]]. When attacking, sometimes it triggered a targeting [[laser/Legends|laser]].

It reached the planet one night when the network of sixteen [[smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]-operated [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] were disabled.
[[File:Grif_Grawley.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Grif Grawley]] aiming at PD 4786.]]
Colonist [[Grif Grawley]] spotted it while looking for [[Bouncer]]s with his [[electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] from [[Hill_461]], but its heat signature and elevation indicated it was a droid and not a Bouncer. He attempted to disable it with his [[blaster rifle/Legends|blast rifle]] but missed his first shot; the second slagged one of its [[sensor/Legends|sensors]], when it retaliated, destroying part of his [[viewport/Legends|windshield]]; he dodged the speeder away from the second shot.

Eventually he destroyed the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] by remotely crashing his [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] into it. The droid remained in the same location trying to hit on the incoming speeder until they collided in an explosion. Grawley retrieved the probot's ID plate and brought it to the [[Smuggler's Rest (Ruusan)|Smuggler's Rest]] [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] in an attempt to warn [[Fort Nowhere]] of the possibility of an [[Battle of Fort Nowhere|impending Imperial attack]].
